name: Deepak Shenoy
notes: Advocates focusing on earnings growth and structural domestic flows (SIPs) rather than short-term FII outflows.

name: Nilesh Shah
notes: Advises multi-asset allocation and moderate return expectations; cautions on high small-cap valuations.

name: Saurabh Mukherjea
notes: Advocates manufacturing exports shift, defensive large-cap compounding, and global diversification via GIFT City.

name: Tuhin Kanta Pandey
notes: Warns of speculative risks in weekly index options and retail losses; monitoring STT hike impact.

name: Sanjay Malhotra
notes: Maintains 5.25% repo rate and neutral policy stance; monitors inflation risks and forex volatility.

name: Devina Mehra
notes: Constructive on bottoming market; favors sector rotation (auto, power) and global asset diversification.

name: Radhika Gupta
notes: Advocates multi-asset 'dal-chaval' investing, multi-cap approach, realistic returns (11-12%), and gold/silver.

name: Samir Arora
notes: Employs elimination strategy to avoid permanent capital loss; targets 15% Nifty returns driven by earnings.

name: Raamdeo Agrawal
notes: Bullish on long-term India (Ferrari); favors large-caps, PSU banks, and QGLP quality-growth framework.

name: Sunil Singhania
notes: Views stagnation as healthy time-correction; expects double-digit earnings growth; favors financials and manufacturing.

name: Ridham Desai
notes: Bullish on domestic cyclicals; projects Sensex target of 95,000 by Dec 2026; warns of global growth slowdown.

name: Hiren Ved
notes: GARP approach targeting 'Leaders of Tomorrow'; advocates buying on panic dips; favors power, NBFCs, and autos.

name: Vikas Khemani
notes: Cautions against FOMO; expects double-digit earnings growth; favors large-cap banks and manufacturing.

name: Nitesh Khandelwal
notes: Proponent of systematic, rule-based algorithmic trading; focuses on AI tools and data API access.

name: Alok Jain
notes: Advocates mechanical, emotion-free momentum investing using model portfolios and systematic rebalancing.
